subject: Geography
title: Ice Caves of Iceland
description: A short lesson to help you apply Ice Caves of Iceland.

hook.text: Beneath Iceland's glaciers lie caves of blue ice so vivid they look like frozen sapphires.

buildup.text: Meltwater carves tunnels through the glacier's base during summer, then freezes in winter.

discovery.text: The ice glows blue because it absorbs red light and transmits only the shorter blue wavelengths.

twist.text: These caves are temporary—they collapse and reform each year as the glacier moves.

climax.text: Every ice cave is unique and fleeting. No two seasons create the same one.

punchline.text: The most beautiful caves on Earth exist for only one season.

quiz.question: Why do ice caves appear blue?
options: The ice absorbs red light and transmits blue wavelengths
Minerals in the ice give it color
Reflected sky light enters the cave
